data.title: Register new handler modules in .test-index
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Overview
After all handler extraction tasks complete, add .test-index entries mapping each new handler module to its associated test file(s). This satisfies epic success criterion sc-6: ".test-index entries created for all new handler modules before epic closure."

## Context
The test gate uses .test-index to know which tests to run when a source file changes. Without entries, any change to a new handler module triggers the full test suite. The .test-index format is: source-file-path: test-file-path (tab-separated, one per line).

## New Modules to Index
After extraction tasks complete, enumerate:
1. ls plugins/dso/scripts/bridge/*.py — handlers from bridge-inbound.py (5ee9-8591) and bridge-outbound.py (9f0d-860d)
2. ls plugins/dso/scripts/ticket/*.py — processors from ticket-reducer.py (f046-3235)
3. Any new Python modules created by c43c-12c8 (split large Python files)

Skip __init__.py files — they are package initializers, not handler modules.

## Implementation
1. Enumerate all .py files in scripts/bridge/ and scripts/ticket/ (excluding __init__.py)
2. For each module, find its associated test file using fuzzy-match or existing .test-index entries for the parent script (e.g., bridge-inbound.py maps to tests/scripts/test-inbound-bridge-push-retry.sh)
3. Add .test-index entries in format: `<source-path>	<test-path>`
4. Verify the entries are syntactically correct (colon-separated, valid paths)

## Acceptance Criteria

Unit test exemption (unit exemption criterion 3: static configuration file — .test-index is not executable code; any test would be a change-detector verifying file content only).

- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] At least one .test-index entry exists for bridge handler modules
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && grep -q 'scripts/bridge/' .test-index
- [ ] At least one .test-index entry exists for ticket processor modules
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && grep -q 'scripts/ticket/' .test-index
- [ ] Each new handler module in scripts/bridge/ has a .test-index entry (excluding __init__.py)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && for f in plugins/dso/scripts/bridge/*.py; do bn=$(basename "$f"); [ "$bn" = "__init__.py" ] && continue; grep -q "$bn" .test-index && echo "OK: $bn" || { echo "MISSING: $bn"; exit 1; }; done
- [ ] Each new processor module in scripts/ticket/ has a .test-index entry (excluding __init__.py)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && for f in plugins/dso/scripts/ticket/*.py; do bn=$(basename "$f"); [ "$bn" = "__init__.py" ] && continue; grep -q "$bn" .test-index && echo "OK: $bn" || { echo "MISSING: $bn"; exit 1; }; done
